title: TC016-Admin can view vendor list and open the Create User page
description: Verifies an authenticated admin can access the admin list and navigate to the user creation form.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (per explicit test step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the corrected admin email into the email field, type the password into the password field, then click the 'Sign In' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('aitbenalihoucine14@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('houcine houcine')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Créer un Utilisateur' button (element [1081]) to open the user creation form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/div/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Admin').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- User creation form not displayed: navigating to /en/admin/create returned a 404 page with message 'This page could not be found.'
- No user-creation form fields or modal content (e.g., 'Nom', 'Prénom', 'Email', 'Password', 'Rôle', 'Créer', 'Enregistrer', 'Annuler') were present on the page.
- Clicking 'Créer un Utilisateur' did not open a modal or navigate to a valid form page; the create route appears broken or missing in this environment.
- Admin creation UI endpoint appears unavailable or mis-routed (404) for the tested URL.

title: TC017-Admin can create a Vendor user and see it appear in the admin list
description: Verifies the end-to-end flow to create a vendor user and confirm it is visible in the vendor list after creation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button in the header to open the login page (use element index 93).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/nav/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:3000/login as the test step requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and click the Sign In button to authenticate.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('aitbenalihoucine14@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('houcine houcine')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin using explicit navigate to http://localhost:3000/admin (test step requires direct navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Créer un Utilisateur' (Create User) button to open the user creation page (use element index 1551).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/div/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/admin/create' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- /admin/create route returned a 404 page; the user creation page could not be reached.
- 'Créer un Utilisateur' button clicks did not open the creation form or navigate to the expected '/admin/create' route.
- Could not select the 'Vendor' role because the creation page was unavailable.

title: TC018-Admin can complete Vendor user creation successfully
description: Verifies the create form can be filled for a Vendor and submitted to show a visible success state or return to the vendor list.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments

            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (must use exact path http://localhost:3000/login as specified).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'aitbenalihoucine14@gmail.com' into the email field (index 264).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('aitbenalihoucine14@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('houcine houcine')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Open the create-user form by clicking the 'Créer un Utilisateur' quick action button (index 762). ASSERTION: The 'Créer un Utilisateur' button (index 762) is present and should open the create form when clicked.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/div/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate explicitly to http://localhost:3000/admin/create (use exact path) to check whether the create-user form is available at that route or to confirm the feature is missing.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin/create", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Créer un Utilisateur').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/admin/create' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Vendor Successfully Added!').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

title: TC019-Admin can create an Admin user with is_admin enabled
description: Verifies creating an admin user by selecting Admin role and enabling is_admin, then confirming a success state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (required explicit navigation step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields with the admin credentials, then click the Sign In button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('aitbenalihoucine14@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('houcine houcine')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin/create (explicit navigation step from the test plan) and inspect the page for role selection and the 'Is admin' control.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin/create", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Admin Successfully Added!').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:

            await browser.close()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        # Input text

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)

title: TC022-Non-admin user cannot access /admin and is denied or redirected
description: Verifies an authenticated non-admin cannot view the platform admin page and is either redirected or sees an access denied message.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button (element 92) to open the login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/nav/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use the exact path http://localhost:3000/login as requested).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the vendor email into the email field and password into the password field, then click the Sign In button to authenticate (use vendor credentials aitbenalihoucine18@gmail.com / Houcine18).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('aitbenalihoucine18@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Houcine18')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin (http://localhost:3000/admin) as the next step (explicit navigate to the path required by the test).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Check whether an "Access denied" message exists on the page content (feature may not exist).
        content = await frame.content()
        if "Access denied" in content:
            print("Found 'Access denied' text on the page.")
        else:
            # Report that the access denied message is not present and mark the task as done.
            print("ISSUE: 'Access denied' text not found on the page. The feature may not exist for non-admin users.")
        # Verify the user was redirected to the dashboard (or is on a dashboard-like page).
        assert "/dashboard" in frame.url, f"Expected '/dashboard' in URL but got: {frame.url}"
        # As an additional confirmation that the user is on the dashboard, assert a dashboard-specific element is visible.
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/nav/a[1]')
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Expected dashboard link 'Vue d'ensemble' to be visible, but it is not."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC023-Admin can see moderation controls on the vendor list

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button (element index 92) to open the login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/nav/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (explicit navigate step from the test plan).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the admin email into the email field, type the password into the password field, then click the Sign In button to log in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('aitbenalihoucine14@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('houcine houcine')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:3000/admin as no suitable on-page link to Admin was found).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the admin vendor list page (/en/admin/vendors) so the page can be inspected for 'Vendors'/'Vendeurs', 'Moderation', and the 'Create' control.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/en/admin/vendors", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Vendors').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Moderation').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[normalize-space(text())="Create"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
